title: Claw Artifact Bundle — evidence.jsonl line
description: Schema for one line (JSON object) in a claw's evidence.jsonl. Each line is a single extracted claim with full provenance.

id.description: Unique claim identifier within this bundle. Use a simple sequential slug (e.g. claim-001).

claim.description: Precise, falsifiable statement of what was found. Should be a complete sentence. Avoid vague language.
examples: Studio v0 uses tmux sessions as the supervisor primitive.
Claws are intended to run in ephemeral git worktrees.

source.description: URL or source key (from sources.toml) where the evidence was found. Must be specific enough to relocate the evidence.

support.description: Verbatim quote or close paraphrase of the supporting evidence with enough surrounding context to verify without re-reading the source. Cite file paths or sections where applicable.
examples: bin/cgl-supervisor shows tmux new-session calls; docs/concepts.md states 'Supervisor — persistent Claude session for a lab'.

confidence.description: Confidence level. low = circumstantial or single ambiguous signal. medium = multiple corroborating signals or clear but non-definitive source. high = directly stated with no ambiguity, or multiple independent confirmations.
